svg.title: IBM life-cycle v2: initial conditions and four sequential stages per generation
svg.desc: Flowchart showing male and female initial distributions (trait z, preference p, choosiness s) and the four life-history stages: viability selection, mate assessment and network formation, reproduction with sperm competition, and carrying capacity with generational turnover.

g.text: Unfit males removed · survivors enter the mating pool
Females sample up to A_max surviving males sequentially
Copulation probability P_ij set by preference function and σ_p
Female fecundity ∝ network degree (direct fitness benefit)
Paternity via fair raffle among network partners
z_k = (z_sire + z_dam) / 2 + ε, ε ~ N(0, σ_ε = 0.2)
All offspring pool into a juvenile cohort
Random mortality → exactly N = 1,000 ♂ and N = 1,000 ♀

svg.text: All adults from generation t die
